title: Emails & Activities tracking emails on multiple items with the same email address
excerpt:
Emails & Activities tracks incoming and outgoing emails on all items that have the same email address in the email column. 
Has anyone found a workaround to only have the emails show up on the item associated with the em…

posts.author: justn (Justin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Emails & Activities tracks incoming and outgoing emails on all items that have the same email address in the email column.

Has anyone found a workaround to only have the emails show up on the item associated with the email thread, and not tracking the email on every other item that has the same email in the email column?

posts.author: mactoph (Christopher Phillips)
posts.cookedHtml: Unfortunately we have not - the Emails & Activities app tracks emails across all boards - even if the board is private. It would be great to see this fixed, but as far as I know it currently isn’t possible.

posts.author: ColinTitmuss (Colin Titmuss)
posts.cookedHtml: I would like to see this feature supported as well. Many of our deals are through partners so many have common contacts, and this makes it hard to find the correspondence related to the specific deal.

posts.author: MaxArceneaux (Max)
posts.cookedHtml: Defitenaly agree with Colin, this seems like a pretty fundamental feature of a crm on the caliber of Monday

posts.author: JorgeLopez (Jorge Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi all, I have been searching for this answer since yesterday. I had been tasked to create a ticketing board where users would email us and the email would trigger a new item to be created. We would then use “Emails & Activities” to communicate with the user until their issue is resolved. The biggest problem we have encountered during testing is that if a user has more than one ticket/item on the board, the system updates all threads in the items associated to their email address. It seems like such a basic feature that I was convinced I was missing it somehow. I have been researching for two days! How is there not an option to automate this?

posts.author: mactoph (Christopher Phillips)
posts.cookedHtml:
I haven’t had a chance to test it yet, but I did come across an an Email & Activities filter called “Item related activities”:

item-related-activities1394×816 74.3 KB

That sounds like it may do what we are hoping for in this thread and may be worth a try.

posts.author: sescott (Steve Escott)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have just tested the “Item Related Activities” filter. These are my findings:

Sent emails

  • It definitely works when an email is sent out of Emails & Activities in that Monday item
  • It didn’t work for an email delivered straight out of Gmail, even though the item ID# was in the Subject.

Received Emails

  • It did not work for a reply to the aforementioned emails when no ID# was included in the thread.
  • It worked when they reply to an email that was sent to them from Emails & Activities inside of the item, as long as it includes the Item ID#. Their reply then goes into the item in a way that it is shown with the “Item Related Activities” filter turned on.
  • When a contact emails me after they insert the item ID# themselves into the Subject header, it did not work, even if replying to an email sent from the item’s Email & Activities.

As you can see, this was not an exhaustive test. From what I understand, monday.com has a team of people focused on Emails & Activities - I hope that they see what I have written above, and work on making this a more reliable feature.

Steve Escott
steve@alldayit.com
Authorized monday.com Partner
AllDayIT.com

posts.author: DanJones (Dan)
posts.cookedHtml: I feel as though a massive feature like emails & Activities should work with the same email address. If it would allow me to add an email to a column for it to monitor and then create a new task each time the subject line is different then it would make it ten times easier.

posts.author: Ace (Asa Nadeau)
posts.cookedHtml:
I found a work-around by assigning a faux email that goes into a Google group (adding a plus sign and anything after is allowed in gmail and will go to that same email…eg “groupemail+custom@domain.com”)

If you had a products table, you could have a products@company.com Google group. Each product could have a unique email like products+prod12@company.com. All emails will go to same group inbox.

posts.author: greggoad (Greg)
posts.cookedHtml:
Being able to log emails based on thread is a critical CRM feature that is currently missing from Monday.

For our purposes the emails and activities functionality is almost useless. Most of our contacts are simultaneously associated with multiple items / deals. If an email is sent from a contact that is on multiple items, that email is updated to all the items even ones that are irrelevant. This is useless. The available filtering options do not solve this issue.

We need a way to associate specific email threads/subjects with an item so that only relevant information is updated to relevant items/deals.

One way to do this would be a feature within the contacts board to manually assign a thread to a specific item. When a new email is received from a contact that is associated with multiple items the user could have the option to assign that thread to a specific item.

Even if there was the ability to manually select specific emails from a contact to be posted as an update to an item that would be helpful and seems like would be an easy feature to implement as a stopgap measure.

For applications where a business works with the same contact from a vendor or contractor across multiple projects simultaneously this functionality is critical.

I hope somebody at Monday is reading this. I have not been able to get anywhere on this issue despite multiple support requests.

Thank you,

Greg

posts.author: Brendon_CN (Brendon O'Sullivan (www.conferencenational.com.au))
posts.cookedHtml: Agreed. Our team pretty much ignore using emails & activities for the same reason, which is a shame because if we could just manually assign a contact to the thread our team would love it!

posts.author: shayan.moussawi (Shayan Moussawi)
posts.cookedHtml:
Is there really not a solution to this issue?

We should be able to filter out email threads that were sent from a particular item.

From my understanding the filter „sent from this item“ should achieve exactly that, but at the moment it simply does not seem to work.

Associating E-Mail Threads regardless of E-Mail to an item is a very vital CRM and Service feature. I am surprised to see that it looks so buggy in Monday.

posts.author: klaasmm (Klaas Mertens)
posts.cookedHtml:
We have a similar issue - having the ability to “pin” or “attach” certain e-mail thread to a certain item would help tremendeously, especially if some conversations are not initiated from the Email & Activities app in monday.com

Definitly a feature request for the future.
And you might want to look at how hive.com has done that - they provide the full overview of the linked mailbox and allow you to attach a specific thread id to a task - that would be perfect to have for Monday.com as well.
See feature description from hive.com here:
Hive Mail: Fully-integrated email within Hive | Hive Help

posts.author: shayan.moussawi (Shayan Moussawi)
posts.cookedHtml: You can actually pin Activities to the top

posts.author: klaasmm (Klaas Mertens)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes, but only if the conversation in question is already in the Emails & Activities list of the specific item - and not filtered out by a filter.
But what i want is to “link”, “pin” or “add” a specific conversation from a mailbox in monday.com to a specific item.
Check out the link i posted regarding hive.com - it explains exactly, what i want to do!

posts.author: JoshuaShaw (Joshua Shaw)
posts.cookedHtml:
It’s been 2.5 years.

Does anyone on the monday team have an answer for whether this basic CRM feature is planned or not?

posts.author: MRad (M Rad)
posts.cookedHtml:
Activities need to be able to be assigned to a particular record. This feature is essential for the CRM, especially where using the automated Outlook integration as the filter if it works only works for emails generated from within the E&A app itself. As the activities logged in the E&A app universal across all workspaces, this should be of attaching the activity to a record ID.
Definitely a deal breaker for us.

posts.author: S_Miller (Sherri Miller)
posts.cookedHtml:
Continuing the discussion from Emails & Activities tracking emails on multiple items with the same email address:

I’m fairly new to Monday and am running up against this same issue. I found a video that seems to provide a good solution:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=kSI5D0MFT6U

Even though it worked perfectly in the video, it doesn’t work for me in that {pulse.item_id} is entered in my email signature template but doesn’t show up in the email, so the automation doesn’t work.

Does this work for anyone and/or can let me know what I’m doing wrong?

posts.author: Ben_psi (Ben Liu)
posts.cookedHtml: I am hoping this feature can add to the road map. Essentially we need a feature similar to the “label” in Gmail and each label can be associated to either an item or group?

posts.author: MassielGonzalez (Massiel Gonzalez)
posts.cookedHtml:
I also am finding the need to assign an email to a Deal in Emails & Activities to appear only in one Deal. I wonder if there is a way to have a drop down within Emails & Activities to allow us to click which Deals we would like the email to show up in the widget.

Maybe one that includes “Show in All Deals” then a list of where the email is associated in other Deals. Similar to the “Set a Reminder” drop down arrow (Pictured)

Screenshot 2025-06-10 at 10.52.06 AM477×335 21.5 KB

Another temporary solution that would also solve this issue would be to be able to “Hide” an activity only for a certain Deal. That way we could at least manually remove it from the Widget.

Currently, if we were to delete one Call Log in one Deal, it would delete on all Deals.

Any other work arounds would be helpful!

posts.author: noam.lapidot (Noam Lapidot)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey all,

There is easy solution for that, there’s an app named SuperMail that functions similar to emails and activities with few bonus features:

  1. It assigns incoming emails to items per item ID rather than the email column so that solves the main issue raised in this discussion.
  2. It allow sending rich text designed automated emails with attachments and hyperlinks (not available in standard monday functionality)
  3. You can include button in email templates that will trigger a status change in your boards. Great for approval process.

You can try for free here:
https://auth.monday.com/oauth2/authorize?client_id=70fcb424f8d669ad26da13c275fb2f62&response_type=install

posts.author: rachel.evans (Rachel Evans)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Noam,

Thanks for this, would this feature be available on the free plan or is there a subscription element to incorporate the item ID and assign to incoming emails?

Thanks

Rachel

posts.author: noam.lapidot (Noam Lapidot)
posts.cookedHtml: @rachel.evans It’s available on the free plan. The limit is on sent emails

posts.author: Mariia (Mariia Mykhasiuk)
posts.cookedHtml:
hi @justn, you’re right - Emails & Activities logs incoming/outgoing messages on every item that contains the same email address, and unfortunately there’s no native way to limit this to a single item/thread.

If you want more control, you might consider using Email to Tasks for monday.com instead.
It lets you send only the specific emails you choose into monday and attach them to exactly the item you want - without syncing the same message across all items that share that email address.

You can log an email as a new item, an update, or a comment, and incoming emails won’t automatically spill onto unrelated records.
